page: Amazon Web Services c6gd.2xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by an AWS Graviton2 processor using the 64-bit ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The memory configuration includes 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. For local storage, the instance is bundled with a 474 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Network connectivity is rated at a 2 Gbps baseline.

Benchmark data reveals average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 12,133.14 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 5,364.0. Single-core performance is weaker, as indicated by a Geekbench single-core score of 930.0 and a poor bzip3 single-threaded decompression rate of 6.93 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is robust for cached reads at 473.11 GB/sec, but drops to 116.89 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The server handles database operations and static web serving with average efficiency, achieving 9.43 million operations per second on Redis SET and 9.78 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture provides a high physical core density relative to its resource footprint, though it lacks x86-specific instruction sets like AVX or AMX. The inclusion of a 474 GB NVMe SSD local storage volume offsets the need for external storage attachments for high-speed scratch space. However, single-threaded workloads and LLM inference tasks show poor performance, with text generation on a 135M model reaching only 38.46 tokens per second. This server is suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and database workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores and local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.2xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 server featuring 8 dedicated physical cores on the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 architecture. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, a 474 GB local NVMe SSD, and a 2 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 12,133.14 and a Redis SET throughput of 9.43 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 930.0. The server lacks x86 vector extensions but offers high physical core density and integrated local storage. It is suitable for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated physical cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 474 GB NVMe SSD, delivering average multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 8 physical cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 474 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and integrated local NVMe storage for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated physical ARM64 cores running at 2.5 GHz
16.0 GB of RAM and 474 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Average multi-core performance with 12,133.14 stress-ng score
Weak single-core and LLM inference performance
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and web serving
